# OHZI Core — LLM Reference

## What is OHZI Core?

OHZI Core is a TypeScript WebGL/WebGPU graphics library built on top of [Three.js](https://threejs.org/). It is **not meant to be used standalone** — it is part of the [OHZI Boilerplate](https://github.com/ohzinteractive/boilerplate), which provides the full project scaffolding.

The library ships as an ES module (`build/index.mjs`) with TypeScript declarations (`types/index.d.ts`). It wraps and extends Three.js with a structured framework for:

- Application lifecycle management
- Rendering pipeline control (render modes, post-processing)
- Scene and camera management
- Asset loading and caching
- Viewport and timing utilities
- Input handling
- Debug visualization

All classes are exported from the top-level package. Import them directly:

```ts
import { Graphics, RenderLoop, Time, OScreen, SceneManager, CameraManager, BaseApplication } from '@ohzi/core';
```

---

## Core Concepts

### Singletons vs Instantiable Classes

Many core systems are **singletons** — exported as a single shared instance. They must be initialized once at startup via `Initializer.init()`.

Singletons: `Graphics`, `Time`, `OScreen`, `SceneManager`, `CameraManager`, `ResourceContainer`, `Debug`, `OS`, `Browser`, `Capabilities`, `TransitionManager`, `ViewManager`, `ViewComponentManager`.

Instantiable: `BaseApplication`, `PerspectiveCamera`, `OrthographicCamera`, `KeyboardInput`, `RenderLoop`, `AbstractScene` (subclasses), and all materials, loaders, components, etc.

### Application Startup Sequence

```ts
// 1. Initialize all core singletons
Initializer.init(input);

// 2. Initialize the renderer (separate because it needs a DOM element and DPR)
Graphics.init({ renderer, core_attributes, dpr: window.devicePixelRatio });

// 3. Create camera and assign it
const camera = new PerspectiveCamera(60, OScreen.aspect_ratio, 0.1, 1000);
CameraManager.current = camera;

// 4. Set a render mode
Graphics.set_state(new NormalRender());

// 5. Create and start the render loop with your application
const app = new MyApplication();
const loop = new RenderLoop(app, Graphics, input);
loop.start();
```

### Teardown

```ts
Initializer.dispose(loop);
// This calls: Graphics.dispose() → SceneManager.dispose() → loop.dispose()
```

---

## RenderLoop

The central frame driver. It calls lifecycle hooks on a `BaseApplication` instance every frame.

```ts
const loop = new RenderLoop(app, Graphics, input);
loop.start();   // begins requestAnimationFrame via renderer.setAnimationLoop
loop.stop();    // calls app.on_exit(), detaches animation loop
loop.dispose(); // stop + reset frame counter (allows full restart)
loop.set_state(newApp); // swap apps mid-run (calls on_exit/on_enter)
```

**Frame order each tick:**

1. `Time.__update()` — update delta/elapsed
2. `app.on_post_start()` — called once on frame 2
3. `input.update()` — process raw events
4. `app.before_update()`
5. Fixed-timestep loop: `app.fixed_update()` (0–N times)
6. `Time.__set_frame_interpolation()`
7. `app.update()`
8. `app.on_pre_render()`
9. `Graphics.update()` — main scene render
10. `app.on_post_render()`
11. `Debug.render()` — debug overlay
12. `app.on_frame_end()`
13. `input.clear()` / `Debug.clear()`

---

## BaseApplication

Extend this to implement your application logic. All methods are no-ops by default.

```ts
class MyApp extends BaseApplication
{
  on_enter(loop: RenderLoop)
  {
    // Called once when this app becomes active
  }

  update()
  {
    // Called every frame after fixed updates
    // Use Time.dt for frame-rate-independent movement
  }

  fixed_update()
  {
    // Called at a fixed rate (default: 30 Hz)
    // Good for physics and deterministic logic
  }

  on_pre_render()  { /* before Graphics renders */ }
  on_post_render() { /* after Graphics renders */ }
  on_frame_end()   { /* very end of frame */ }
  on_exit(loop)    { /* cleanup when leaving this state */ }
}
```

---

## Graphics

Singleton that wraps the Three.js WebGPU `Renderer`. Manages rendering, post-processing, resize, and screenshots.

```ts
// Render a scene manually
Graphics.render(scene, camera, renderTarget, overrideMaterial);

// Full-screen post-process blit pass
Graphics.blit(srcRT, dstRT, material);
Graphics.material_pass(mat, dstRT);

// Switch render mode (e.g., NormalRender → UnrealBloomRender)
Graphics.set_state(new UnrealBloomRender());

// Screenshot (tiled, high-res)
Graphics.take_screenshot((blob) => Graphics.download_screenshot(blob), 3840, 2160);

// Read back GPU pixels to CPU
const pixels = await Graphics.readback_RT(renderTarget);
```

**Key properties:**

| Property | Description |
|----------|-------------|
| `Graphics._renderer` | The raw Three.js `Renderer` instance |
| `Graphics.canvas` | The `HTMLCanvasElement` |
| `Graphics.current_render_mode` | The active `BaseRender` instance |
| `Graphics.blitter` | `Blitter` for blit passes |
| `Graphics.depth_and_normals_renderer` | Generates depth/normal G-buffer |
| `Graphics.generate_depth_normal_texture` | Set `true` to enable depth/normal pass each frame |

---

## Render Modes

Render modes are classes extending `BaseRender` that control how the scene is drawn each frame. Switch between them via `Graphics.set_state()`.

Available render modes:

| Class | Description |
|-------|-------------|
| `NormalRender` | Standard clear → render pipeline |
| `UnrealBloomRender` | Bloom post-processing |
| `NormalAORender` | Normal + AO pass |
| `VRRender` | WebXR stereo rendering |
| `DebugNormalsRender` | Visualize scene normals |

Custom render modes extend `BaseRender` and implement `render()`:

```ts
class MyRender extends BaseRender
{
  render()
  {
    Graphics.clear(undefined, CameraManager.current, true, true);
    SceneManager.current.on_pre_render();
    SceneManager.current.render();
    // post-process: Graphics.blit(srcRT, dstRT, myMaterial)
    SceneManager.current.on_post_render();
  }
}
```

---

## Time

Singleton for all frame-timing values. Read in `update()` or `fixed_update()`.

```ts
Time.dt            // delta_time in seconds (current frame)
Time.sdt           // smooth_delta_time (10-frame rolling average)
Time.elapsed_time  // total seconds since app start
Time.frame_interpolation // 0–1 fraction between fixed steps (use for visual interpolation)
Time.fixed_delta_time    // fixed step interval, default 1/30 s (writable)
```

---

## OScreen

Singleton for viewport/canvas dimensions and DPR. Updated automatically by `Graphics` on resize.

```ts
OScreen.width          // CSS logical width
OScreen.height         // CSS logical height
OScreen.render_width   // physical pixels (width * dpr)
OScreen.render_height  // physical pixels (height * dpr)
OScreen.aspect_ratio   // width / height
OScreen.dpr            // device pixel ratio
OScreen.pixel_size     // Vector2(1/width, 1/height) — useful in shaders
OScreen.size_changed   // true for one frame when a resize occurred
OScreen.portrait       // true when width < height
```

---

## SceneManager

Singleton holding the active Three.js scene.

```ts
SceneManager.current = myScene; // set active scene
SceneManager.current;           // read active scene
SceneManager.dispose();         // dispose all geometries/materials in current scene
```

The rendering pipeline (`NormalRender`, etc.) always reads `SceneManager.current` as the default scene.

---

## CameraManager

Singleton holding the active camera.

```ts
CameraManager.current = new PerspectiveCamera(60, OScreen.aspect_ratio, 0.1, 1000);
CameraManager.current;   // read active camera
CameraManager.spectator; // secondary VR spectator camera
```

`Graphics.update()` syncs `CameraManager.current.aspect` to `OScreen.aspect_ratio` automatically each frame.

---

## PerspectiveCamera / OrthographicCamera

Thin extensions of their Three.js counterparts adding `clear_color` and `clear_alpha` per camera.

```ts
const cam = new PerspectiveCamera(60, OScreen.aspect_ratio, 0.1, 1000);
cam.clear_color = new Color('#1a1a2e');
cam.clear_alpha = 1;
CameraManager.current = cam;
```

---

## AbstractScene

Abstract base class for scenes. Extend it to define assets, setup logic, and per-frame hooks. Implements a **two-phase loading pipeline**: regular quality → high quality.

```ts
class MyScene extends AbstractScene
{
  constructor()
  {
    super({ name: 'my_scene', compilators: { SceneCompilator, TexturesCompilator, AudiosCompilator } });
    this.set_assets(sceneObjects, sceneTextures, sceneSounds);
    this.set_high_assets(hqObjects, hqTextures, []);
  }

  init()
  {
    super.init();
    // One-time setup: add lights, meshes, etc.
  }

  on_assets_ready()
  {
    // Regular assets downloaded — access via ResourceContainer.get('name')
  }

  on_assets_compiled()
  {
    super.on_assets_compiled(); // required: triggers high-quality load
    // Assets compiled and GPU-ready
  }

  on_high_quality_assets_ready()
  {
    // High-quality assets done
  }

  on_pre_render()
  {
    // Update uniforms, animate objects, etc.
  }
}
```

**Loading flow:** `scene.load()` → loaders run in a web worker → `on_assets_ready()` → compilators run (spread across frames) → `on_assets_compiled()` → high-quality load starts → `on_high_quality_assets_ready()`.

**State flags:**

| Property | Meaning |
|----------|---------|
| `scene.is_loaded` | Regular assets compiled and ready |
| `scene.is_high_loaded` | High-quality assets loaded |
| `scene.loading_progress` | 0–1 loading progress |

---

## ResourceContainer

Singleton asset cache. Prevents duplicate loading of the same URL.

```ts
// Write (done automatically by loaders)
ResourceContainer.set_resource('hero_tex', '/assets/hero.png', loadedTexture);

// Read (use in on_assets_ready / on_assets_compiled)
const tex = ResourceContainer.get('hero_tex');
```

Two different names pointing to the same URL resolve to the same object instance.

---

## Asset Loaders

### Batch async loaders (used by AbstractScene)

`AsyncObjectsLoader`, `AsyncTexturesLoader`, `AsyncAudiosLoader` — load arrays of asset descriptors in a shared web worker.

### Individual loaders

All extend `AbstractLoader` with a `load(name, url)` → `Promise` pattern:

| Loader | Asset type |
|--------|-----------|
| `GLTFLoader` | `.gltf` / `.glb` |
| `GLTFDRACOLoader` | DRACO-compressed GLTF |
| `TextureLoader` | Images |
| `AsyncTextureLoader` | Images (async, via ImageBitmap) |
| `HDRTextureLoader` | `.hdr` equirectangular |
| `HDRCubeTextureLoader` | HDR cubemaps |
| `CubemapLoader` | Regular cubemaps |
| `BasisLoader` | `.basis` compressed textures |
| `AudioLoader` | Audio files |
| `FontLoader` | Three.js JSON fonts |
| `OBJLoader` | `.obj` meshes |
| `DAELoader` | Collada `.dae` |
| `VideoLoader` | Video textures |
| `FileLoader` | Raw file fetch |
| `TextLoader` | Text files |
| `JSONLoader` | JSON files |
| `PointArrayLoader` | Point array data |

---

## Materials

### BaseShaderMaterial

Base class for all custom shader materials. Extends Three.js `ShaderMaterial`.

```ts
class MyMaterial extends BaseShaderMaterial
{
  constructor()
  {
    super(vertGLSL, fragGLSL, {
      _Color:   { value: new Color('#ffffff') },
      _Texture: { value: null },
      _Time:    { value: 0 }
    });
  }

  update()
  {
    this.uniforms._Time.value = Time.elapsed_time;
  }
}
```

### Built-in materials

`AddMaterial`, `BlitMaterial`, `UnrealBloomComposeMaterial`, `DualFilteringBlurMaterial` — ready-to-use post-processing materials.

---

## Render Utilities

```ts
// Dual-filter blur (efficient, large radius)
const blurrer = new DualFilteringBlurrer();
blurrer.blur(srcRT, dstRT, iterations);

// Gaussian blur
const gaussian = new GaussianBlurrer();
gaussian.blur(srcRT, dstRT, radius);

// Median filter
const median = new MedianFilter();
median.apply(srcRT, dstRT);

// Depth + normals G-buffer
Graphics.generate_depth_normal_texture = true;
// Access via: Graphics.depth_normals_RT
```

---

## Components

Ready-to-use Three.js `Object3D` subclasses:

| Class | Description |
|-------|-------------|
| `Grid` | Infinite ground grid |
| `Line` | Line through 3D points |
| `Text2D` | Screen-space 2D text |
| `WorldImage` | Billboard image in world space |
| `SDFTextBatch` | GPU-batched SDF text rendering |
| `UpdatableMaterialMesh` | Mesh with a per-frame material update hook |

---

## Primitives

```ts
new Cube(size)
new Sphere(radius, segments)
new Arrow(origin, direction, color)
new HorizontalPlane(width, height)
new VerticalPlane(width, height)
```

---

## KeyboardInput

Not a singleton — instantiate as needed.

```ts
const kb = new KeyboardInput();
kb.init(document);
kb.register_key('Space');
kb.register_key('KeyW');

// In update():
if (kb.is_key_pressed('Space'))   { /* triggered once on press */ }
if (kb.is_key_down('KeyW'))       { /* true while held */ }
if (kb.is_key_released('Space'))  { /* triggered once on release */ }

kb.dispose(); // remove listeners
```

---

## Debug

Singleton for development-time overlays. All 3D objects added to a separate debug scene rendered after the main scene. Cleared automatically at frame end.

```ts
// 3D debug shapes (drawn in debug scene)
Debug.draw_arrow(origin, direction, color);
Debug.draw_line([p1, p2, p3], color);
Debug.draw_cube(position, size, color);
Debug.draw_sphere(position, radius, color);
Debug.draw_axis();
Debug.draw_texture(renderTarget.texture, width, height);

// 2D overlay (requires Debug.ctx set to a CanvasRenderingContext2D)
Debug.draw_rectangle(pos2d, width, height, color);
Debug.draw_line_2D(from, to, color);
```

---

## RenderLayers

Named constants for Three.js layer indices:

```ts
RenderLayers.opaque       // 0 — default opaque geometry
RenderLayers.transparent  // 1 — transparent geometry
RenderLayers.outline      // 2 — outline-effect objects
RenderLayers.selectable   // 3 — raycasting-selectable objects

mesh.layers.set(RenderLayers.transparent);
camera.layers.enable(RenderLayers.opaque);
```

---

## OS / Browser / Capabilities

Singletons for platform detection. All flags set once at `Initializer.init()`.

```ts
OS.is_mobile   // true on phones/tablets
OS.is_ios      // true on iPhone/iPad
OS.is_android  // true on Android
OS.is_mac      // true on macOS
OS.is_windows  // true on Windows

Browser.is_chrome
Browser.is_firefox
Browser.is_safari

Capabilities.supports_webgpu
// etc.
```

---

## ActionSequencer

Event sequencing with interpolators — useful for scripted animations and transitions.

```ts
const seq = new ActionSequencer();
seq.add(new ActionEvent(() => { /* instant action */ }));
seq.add(new NumberInterpolator(fromVal, toVal, duration, (v) => { obj.position.x = v; }));
seq.add(new VectorInterpolator(fromVec, toVec, duration, (v) => { obj.position.copy(v); }));
seq.play();
```

---

## View / UI System

`ViewManager`, `ViewComponentManager`, and `ApplicationView` provide a UI layer abstraction above Three.js, with transition animations driven by `TransitionManager`. These are wired into the `RenderLoop` automatically and updated each frame.

---

## GPU Particles

```ts
const system = new GPUParticleSystem(count);
system.add_attribute(new ParticlePositionAttribute(...));
system.add_attribute(new ParticleAttribute('velocity', ...));
scene.add(system.mesh);
// Update in on_pre_render via custom compute shaders
```

---

## Full Minimal Example

```ts
import {
  Initializer, Graphics, RenderLoop, BaseApplication,
  PerspectiveCamera, CameraManager, OScreen, SceneManager,
  NormalRender, Time, AbstractScene
} from '@ohzi/core';
import { WebGPURenderer, AmbientLight, Mesh, BoxGeometry, MeshStandardMaterial } from 'three';

// --- Scene ---
class MyScene extends AbstractScene
{
  constructor()
  {
    super({ name: 'main', compilators: { SceneCompilator, TexturesCompilator, AudiosCompilator } });
  }

  init()
  {
    super.init();
    const mesh = new Mesh(new BoxGeometry(), new MeshStandardMaterial({ color: 0x4488ff }));
    this.add(mesh);
    this.add(new AmbientLight(0xffffff, 1));
  }

  on_pre_render()
  {
    this.children[0].rotation.y += Time.dt;
  }
}

// --- Application ---
class App extends BaseApplication
{
  on_enter()
  {
    const scene = new MyScene();
    scene.init();
    SceneManager.current = scene;

    const cam = new PerspectiveCamera(60, OScreen.aspect_ratio, 0.1, 1000);
    cam.position.set(0, 1, 3);
    CameraManager.current = cam;

    Graphics.set_state(new NormalRender());
  }
}

// --- Bootstrap ---
const renderer = new WebGPURenderer({ antialias: true });
document.body.appendChild(renderer.domElement);

Initializer.init(input);
Graphics.init({ renderer, core_attributes: {}, dpr: window.devicePixelRatio });

const app = new App();
const loop = new RenderLoop(app, Graphics, input);
loop.start();
```
